question: What tables and key columns are available in the database?
sql:
SELECT
  table_name,
  column_name,
  data_type
FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.INFORMATION_SCHEMA.COLUMNS`
WHERE table_name IN ('orders', 'order_items', 'products', 'users')
ORDER BY table_name, ordinal_position
report: The dataset has four core tables. orders (one row per order) holds the order lifecycle and timestamps. order_items (one row per line item) holds sale_price, the source of revenue. products (one row per SKU) holds category, brand, cost, and retail_price. users (one row per customer) holds demographics and location - and the dataset's PII. Join order_items to products on product_id = id, to orders on order_id, and to users on user_id.
